summary: Checkout API returned 500s during evening peak as the Postgres connection pool saturated at max 20 connections; requests queued and timed out waiting for a free connection.
symptoms: active connections pinned at pool max
remediation_applied: Raised HikariCP maximumPoolSize from 20 to 60 and added a 5s connection acquisition timeout with alerting.

summary: Orders service exhausted its MySQL connection pool after a slow analytics query held connections open, starving normal traffic of database handles.
remediation_applied: Killed the runaway analytics query, moved reporting to a read replica, and capped statement timeout at 10s.

summary: A connection leak in the user-service ORM layer failed to release connections back to the pool, gradually exhausting Postgres capacity over several hours.
remediation_applied: Rolling restart to reclaim leaked connections, then patched the ORM code path to close connections in a finally block.

summary: Payments API connection pool exhaustion triggered by a downstream Postgres failover; the pool kept dead connections and could not acquire new ones fast enough.
remediation_applied: Enabled pool connection validation (test-on-borrow) and reduced max lifetime to 5 minutes so stale connections are recycled.

summary: Cart-service saturated its Redis and Postgres connection pools during a flash sale; concurrency far exceeded the configured pool size.
remediation_applied: Scaled out cart-service replicas and increased per-instance pool size, distributing load across more database connections.

summary: Notification-service memory grew unbounded due to an in-memory queue that was never drained, leading to OOM kills and restart loops.
remediation_applied: Rolling restart to relieve pressure, then bounded the in-memory queue and added backpressure to producers.

summary: Search-service heap leaked from an unbounded query result cache with no eviction policy, exhausting the JVM heap and degrading latency.
remediation_applied: Restarted affected pods and switched the cache to an LRU with a hard size cap and TTL eviction.

summary: Api-gateway leaked native memory via a metrics library that accumulated unbounded label cardinality, slowly consuming all container memory.
remediation_applied: Restarted gateway and removed the unbounded label dimension from the metrics instrumentation.

summary: Recommendation-service leaked memory through event listeners that were registered but never removed on each request, growing the retained heap.
remediation_applied: Rolling restart and a code fix to deregister listeners in the request teardown path.

summary: Public API rejected all HTTPS traffic after the TLS certificate for api.example.com expired at midnight UTC; clients received certificate-expired errors.
symptoms: sudden total request failure at 00:00 UTC
remediation_applied: Issued and deployed a renewed certificate via ACME and reloaded the ingress; automated renewal with cert-manager afterward.

summary: Internal service mesh mTLS broke when an intermediate CA certificate expired, cutting service-to-service traffic between payments and orders.
remediation_applied: Rotated the intermediate CA and restarted the mesh control plane to redistribute trust bundles.

summary: A partner webhook integration failed because the client certificate used for mutual TLS expired, blocking outbound event delivery.
remediation_applied: Generated a new client certificate, updated the secret, and restarted the delivery workers.

summary: A misapplied feature-flag config change disabled request caching in production, overloading the origin services and spiking latency.
symptoms: cache hit ratio dropped to near zero
remediation_applied: Reverted the feature-flag change to the last known-good value and re-enabled caching.

summary: Staging config accidentally promoted to production pointed the inventory-service at the staging database, returning stale and incorrect stock levels.
symptoms: service pointing at wrong database host
remediation_applied: Restored the correct production database endpoint in config and redeployed with config validation gates.

summary: An untracked manual change to an environment variable raised the thread pool size beyond host capacity, causing CPU thrash and timeouts.
remediation_applied: Reverted the environment variable to the IaC-defined value and locked down manual edits.

summary: A rate-limit configuration drift set the limit far too low after a bad merge, throttling legitimate traffic and returning 429s to customers.
remediation_applied: Restored the rate-limit value from version control and added a policy test to catch regressions.

summary: Service discovery failed when the internal DNS resolver became unreachable, so services could not resolve database and dependency hostnames.
symptoms: name resolution failures for internal hosts
remediation_applied: Failed over to the secondary DNS resolver and flushed negative caches across the fleet.

summary: A DNS record TTL misconfiguration caused clients to cache a decommissioned load-balancer IP, sending traffic to a black hole after migration.
symptoms: traffic to stale IP after migration
remediation_applied: Lowered the record TTL and restored the old endpoint temporarily until caches expired.

summary: Upstream DNS provider outage caused intermittent resolution failures for external payment gateway hostnames, failing a fraction of transactions.
remediation_applied: Added a secondary DNS provider and increased resolver retry with local caching of successful lookups.

summary: Checkout degraded when the third-party payment gateway slowed dramatically, causing request pile-up and thread starvation in payments-api.
remediation_applied: Enabled a circuit breaker with fallback and tightened upstream timeouts to shed load.

summary: Recommendation quality dropped and latency rose after the upstream feature-store service degraded, returning slow and partial responses.
remediation_applied: Served cached features as a fallback and reduced dependency timeout to fail fast.

summary: Shipping-service errors climbed when an upstream carrier rate API degraded under its own incident, causing timeouts on shipping quote requests.
remediation_applied: Introduced exponential backoff with jitter and a fallback to last-known carrier rates.

summary: A bad deploy of user-service introduced a null-pointer regression on login; error rates spiked immediately after rollout and were fixed by rolling back.
symptoms: regression tied to new release version
remediation_applied: Rolled back to the previous stable release and paused the pipeline pending a fix.

summary: A checkout-service deployment shipped an incompatible database migration that broke reads; the release was rolled back and the migration reverted.
remediation_applied: Rolled back the deployment and reverted the migration, then re-planned a backward-compatible migration.

summary: A new inventory-service release changed default timeouts and caused cascading slowness; rolling back the deployment restored normal behavior.
remediation_applied: Rolled back to the prior release and re-introduced the change behind a flag with load testing.
